facts.fact: The policy is an Allstate Auto Insurance Policy.
The policy form number is AU127-1.
The issuing company is Allstate Insurance Company.
Allstate Insurance Company is a Stock Company.
Allstate Insurance Company's Home Office is located in Northbrook, Illinois.
This policy is a legal contract between the policyholder (you) and Allstate (us).
A coverage applies only when a premium for it is shown on the Policy Declarations.
If more than one auto is insured, premiums will be shown for each auto.
If the policyholder pays premiums when due and complies with policy terms, Allstate makes the stated agreements with the policyholder.
The policy applies only during the policy period.
During the policy period, the policy applies to losses to the auto, accidents and occurrences within the United States of America.
During the policy period, the policy applies to losses to the auto, accidents and occurrences within US territories or possessions.
During the policy period, the policy applies to losses to the auto, accidents and occurrences within Canada.
During the policy period, the policy applies to losses to the auto, accidents and occurrences between the ports of the United States of America, its territories or possessions, and Canada.
The policy period is shown on the Policy Declarations.
The premium for each auto is based on information Allstate has received from the policyholder or other sources.
The policyholder agrees to cooperate with Allstate in determining if premium information is correct, complete, and whether it changes during the policy period.
If premium information changes or is incorrect or incomplete, Allstate may adjust the premium accordingly during the policy period.
Changes which result in a premium adjustment are contained in Allstate's rules.
Premium-adjusting changes include, but are not limited to: autos insured by the policy, including changes in use.
Premium-adjusting changes include, but are not limited to: drivers residing in the policyholder's household, their ages or marital status.
Premium-adjusting changes include, but are not limited to: coverages or coverage limits.
Premium-adjusting changes include, but are not limited to: rating territory.
Premium-adjusting changes include, but are not limited to: discount eligibility.
Any calculation or adjustment of premium will be made using the rules, rates and forms in effect, and on file if required, for Allstate's use in the policyholder's state.
When Allstate broadens a coverage during the policy period without additional charge, the policyholder has the new feature if they have the coverage to which it applies.
A new coverage feature applies on the date the coverage change is effective in the policyholder's state.
Otherwise, the policy can be changed only by endorsement.
Any change in coverage will be made using the rules, rates and forms in effect, and on file if required, for Allstate's use in the policyholder's state.
The policyholder must tell Allstate within 60 days when they acquire an additional or replacement auto.
If the policyholder does not report an additional or replacement auto within 60 days, certain coverages of the policy may not apply.
If the policyholder has two or more autos insured in their name and one of these autos is involved in an accident, only the coverage limits shown on the Policy Declarations for that auto will apply.

When the policyholder has two or more autos insured in their name and none of them is involved in the accident, the policyholder may choose any single auto shown on the Policy Declarations and the coverage limits applicable to that auto will apply.
The limits available for any other auto covered by the policy will not be added to the coverage for the involved or chosen auto.
This policy cannot be transferred to anyone without Allstate's written consent.
If the policyholder dies, coverage will be provided until the end of the policy period for the policyholder's legal representative while acting as such.
If the policyholder dies, coverage will be provided until the end of the policy period for persons covered on the date of the policyholder's death.
The policyholder may cancel this policy by writing Allstate the future date they wish to stop coverage.
Allstate may cancel part or all of the policy by mailing notice to the policyholder at their last known address.
If Allstate cancels because the policyholder did not pay the premium, the date of cancellation will be at least 10 days after the date of mailing.

If Allstate cancels for any reason other than non-payment of premium, during the first 60 days the original policy is in effect, Allstate will give the policyholder at least 10 days notice of cancellation.
If Allstate cancels for any reason other than non-payment of premium, after the first 60 days, Allstate will give the policyholder 30 days notice of cancellation.
Proof of mailing the cancellation notice will be proof of notice.
A refund, if due, will be proportional to the time the policy has been in effect.
Cancellation will be effective even if the refund is not made immediately.
After the original policy has been in effect 60 days, Allstate will not cancel the policy unless: the policyholder does not pay the premium when it is due.

After the original policy has been in effect 60 days, Allstate will not cancel the policy unless: the policyholder or any member of the policyholder's household is convicted of a crime due to acts which increase the hazard Allstate insures against.
After the original policy has been in effect 60 days, Allstate will not cancel the policy unless: fraud or material misrepresentation is used in obtaining the policy or making a claim.

After the original policy has been in effect 60 days, Allstate will not cancel the policy unless: an act or omission, or a violation of any condition of the policy, occurs during the policy period which substantially and materially increases the hazard Allstate insures against.

After the original policy has been in effect 60 days, Allstate will not cancel the policy unless: a material change in the nature or extent of the risk occurs during the policy period which causes the risk of loss to be substantially and materially increased.

After the original policy has been in effect 60 days, Allstate will not cancel the policy unless: Allstate has mailed notice within the first 60 days that Allstate does not intend to continue the policy.
If Allstate does not intend to continue the policy beyond the current policy period, Allstate will mail notice to the policyholder at least 30 days before the end of the policy period.
Under Part 1 (Coverage AA - Bodily Injury), Allstate will pay for damages an insured person is legally obligated to pay because of bodily injury sustained by any person.
Under Part 1 (Coverage BB - Property Damage), Allstate will pay for damages an insured person is legally obligated to pay because of damage to, or destruction of, property.
Under Part 1, the policy protects an insured person from liability for damages arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use, loading or unloading of an insured auto.
Under Part 1, Allstate will defend an insured person sued for damages which are covered by this policy, even if the suit is groundless or false.
Under Part 1, Allstate will choose the counsel when defending an insured person.
Under Part 1, Allstate may settle any claim or suit if Allstate believes it is proper.
Under Part 1, Allstate will not defend an insured person sued for damages which are not covered by this policy.

Under Part 1, when defending an insured person, Allstate will pay up to $50 a day for loss of wages or salary if Allstate asks that person to attend hearings or trials to defend against a bodily injury suit.
Under Part 1, Allstate will not pay for loss of other income (other than wages or salary) incurred in attending hearings or trials.
Under Part 1, Allstate will pay other reasonable expenses incurred at Allstate's request when an insured person attends hearings or trials.
Under Part 1, Allstate will pay court costs for defense.
Under Part 1, Allstate will pay interest accruing on damages awarded, only until Allstate has paid, offered, or deposited in court the amount for which Allstate is liable under the policy.
Under Part 1, Allstate will only pay interest on damages not exceeding Allstate's limits of liability.
Under Part 1, Allstate will pay premiums on appeal bonds and on bonds to release attachments, but not in excess of Allstate's limit of liability.
Under Part 1, Allstate is not required to apply for or furnish appeal bonds or bonds to release attachments.
Under Part 1, Allstate will repay an insured person for the cost of any bail bonds required due to an accident or traffic law violation involving the use of the insured auto, up to $300 per bond.
Under Part 1, Allstate is not required to apply for or furnish bail bonds.
Under Part 1, Allstate will repay an insured person for any expense incurred for first aid to others at the time of an auto accident involving the insured auto.
Under Part 1, insured persons while using the policyholder's insured auto include: the policyholder (you).
Under Part 1, insured persons while using the policyholder's insured auto include: any resident.
Under Part 1, insured persons while using the policyholder's insured auto include: any other person using it with the policyholder's permission.
Under Part 1, insured persons while using a non-owned auto include: the policyholder (you).
Under Part 1, insured persons while using a non-owned auto include: any resident relative using a four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto.

Under Part 1, any other person or organization liable for the use of an insured auto is an insured person if the auto is not owned or hired by this person or organization, provided the use is by an insured person under categories 1 or 2.
Under Part 1, insured autos include any auto described on the Policy Declarations, including the four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto the policyholder replaces it with.

Under Part 1, insured autos include an additional four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto the policyholder becomes the owner of during the policy period, if Allstate insures all other private passenger autos or utility autos the policyholder owns.
Under Part 1, to cover an additional auto during the policy period, the policyholder must tell Allstate within 60 days of acquiring the auto.
Under Part 1, to cover an additional auto during the policy period, the policyholder must pay any additional premium.

Under Part 1, insured autos include a substitute four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto, not owned by the policyholder or a resident, being temporarily used while the policyholder's insured auto is being serviced or repaired.

Under Part 1, insured autos include a substitute four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto, not owned by the policyholder or a resident, being temporarily used if the policyholder's insured auto is stolen or destroyed.

Under Part 1, insured autos include a non-owned auto used by the policyholder or a resident relative with the owner's permission, provided this auto is not available or furnished for the regular use of an insured person.
Under Part 1, insured autos include a trailer while attached to an insured auto, provided the trailer is designed for use with a private passenger auto or utility auto.
Under Part 1, a trailer insured auto cannot be used for business purposes with other than a private passenger auto or utility auto.
Under Part 1, 'Allstate', 'We', 'Us', or 'Our' means the Allstate Insurance Company or Allstate Indemnity Company as shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 1, 'Auto' means a land motor vehicle designed for use on public roads.
Under Part 1, 'Bodily Injury' means bodily injury, sickness, disease or death.
Under Part 1, 'Resident' means a person who physically resides in the policyholder's household with the intention of continuing residence there.
Under Part 1, the policyholder's unmarried dependent children while temporarily away from home will be considered residents if they intend to resume residing in the policyholder's household.
Under Part 1, 'Utility Auto' means an auto of the pick-up body, sedan delivery or panel truck type, with a rated load capacity of not more than 2,000 pounds.
Under Part 1, 'You' or 'Your' means the policyholder named on the Policy Declarations and that policyholder's resident spouse.

Under Part 1 (Exclusion 1), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury or property damage arising out of the use of the policyholder's insured auto while used to carry persons or property for a charge, to the extent limits of liability exceed Nevada financial responsibility law requirements.

Under Part 1 (Exclusion 1), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury or property damage arising out of the use of any auto the policyholder is driving while available for hire by the public, to the extent limits of liability exceed Nevada financial responsibility law requirements.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 1), the exclusion for carrying persons or property for a charge does not apply to shared expense car pools.

Under Part 1 (Exclusion 2), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury or property damage arising out of the use of a non-owned auto in any business or occupation of an insured person, to the extent limits of liability exceed Nevada financial responsibility law requirements.

Under Part 1 (Exclusion 2), the business use exclusion for non-owned autos does not apply while the policyholder, the policyholder's chauffeur, or domestic servant are using a private passenger auto or trailer.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 3), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury to an employee of any insured person arising in the course of employment.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 3), the employee bodily injury exclusion does not apply to the policyholder's domestic employee who is not required to be covered by a workers compensation law or similar law.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 4), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury to a co-worker injured in the course of employment.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 4), the co-worker exclusion does not apply to the policyholder.

Under Part 1 (Exclusion 5), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury to the policyholder or any resident of the policyholder's household related to the policyholder by blood, marriage, or adoption, to the extent limits of liability exceed Nevada financial responsibility law requirements.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 6), Allstate will not pay for damage to or destruction of property an insured person owns, transports, is in charge of, or rents.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 6), a private residence or a garage rented by an insured person is covered despite the property exclusion.

Under Part 1 (Exclusion 7), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury or property damage which may reasonably be expected to result from the intentional acts of an insured person or which are in fact intended by an insured person, to the extent limits of liability exceed Nevada financial responsibility law requirements.

Under Part 1 (Exclusion 8), Allstate will not pay for bodily injury or property damage which would also be covered under nuclear energy liability insurance, to the extent limits of liability exceed Nevada financial responsibility law requirements.
Under Part 1 (Exclusion 8), the nuclear energy exclusion applies even if the limits of that other insurance are exhausted.
Under Part 1, when this policy is certified as proof under any motor vehicle financial responsibility law, this policy will comply with the provisions of that law.
Under Part 1, the limits shown on the Policy Declarations are the maximum Allstate will pay for any single auto accident.

Under Part 1, the limit stated for each person for bodily injury is Allstate's total limit of liability for damages because of bodily injury sustained by one person in any single auto accident, including damages sustained by anyone else as a result of that bodily injury.

Under Part 1, subject to the limit for each person, the limit stated for each occurrence is Allstate's total limit of liability for damages for bodily injury sustained by two or more persons in any single auto accident.
Under Part 1, for property damage, the limit applies to damages arising from each occurrence.
Under Part 1, the liability limits apply to each insured auto as shown on the Policy Declarations.

Under Part 1, the insuring of more than one person or auto under this policy will not increase Allstate's liability limits beyond the amount shown for any one auto, even though a separate premium is charged for each auto.
Under Part 1, the limits will not be increased if the policyholder has other auto insurance policies that apply.
Under Part 1, there will be no duplication of payments made under the bodily injury liability coverage, uninsured motorists coverage and medical payments coverage of this policy.
Under Part 1, an auto and attached trailer are considered one auto.
Under Part 1, an auto and a mounted camper unit, topper, cap or canopy are considered one auto.
Under Part 1, if an insured person is using a substitute private passenger auto or non-owned auto, Allstate's liability insurance will be excess over other collectible insurance.

Under Part 1, if more than one policy applies on a primary basis to an accident involving the policyholder's insured auto, Allstate will bear its proportionate share with other collectible liability insurance.
Under Part 1, this insurance will be excess over any insurance covering an automobile business while the policyholder's insured auto is being operated by a person engaged in that automobile business.
Under Part 1, 'automobile business' includes repairing, servicing, testing, washing, parking, storing, delivering or selling of autos.
Under Part 1, when Allstate asks, an insured person must cooperate with Allstate in the investigation, settlement and defense of any claim or lawsuit.
Under Part 1, if Allstate asks, an insured person must also help Allstate obtain payment from anyone who may be jointly responsible.
Under Part 1, Allstate cannot be obligated if an insured person voluntarily takes any action or makes any payments other than for covered expenses for bail bonds or first aid to others.
Under Part 1, no insured person may sue Allstate under this coverage unless there is full compliance with all the policy terms.

Under Part 1, if liability has been determined by judgment after trial, or by written agreement among the insured, the other person, and Allstate, then whoever obtains this judgment or agreement against an insured person may sue Allstate up to the limits of this policy.
Under Part 1, no one has the right to join Allstate in a suit to determine legal responsibility.
Under Part 1, the bankruptcy or insolvency of an insured person or that person's estate will not relieve Allstate of any obligation.
Under Part 1, if an insured person has an auto accident, Allstate must be informed promptly of all details.
Under Part 1, if an insured person is sued as the result of an auto accident, Allstate must be informed immediately.

Under Part 2 (Coverage CC - Automobile Medical Payments), Allstate will pay to or on behalf of an insured person all reasonable expenses actually incurred by an insured person for necessary medical treatment, services, or products actually provided to the insured person.
Under Part 2, payments will be made only when bodily injury, sickness, disease, or death is caused by an auto accident.

Under Part 2, covered expenses include: ambulance, hospital, medical, surgical, X-ray, dental, orthopedic and prosthetic devices, professional nursing services, pharmaceuticals, eyeglasses, hearing aids, and funeral service expenses.
Under Part 2, the treatment, services, or products must be rendered within one year after the accident.
Under Part 2, the one-year treatment period will be extended to five years if the amount of insurance shown on the Policy Declarations for this coverage is more than $5,000.
Under Part 2, this coverage does not apply to any person to the extent that the treatment is covered under any workers compensation law.
Under Part 2, insured persons include: the policyholder and any resident relative who sustains bodily injury while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck by, an auto or trailer.
Under Part 2, for the policyholder and resident relative coverage, the use of a non-owned auto must be with the owner's permission.

Under Part 2, insured persons include any other person who sustains bodily injury while in, on, getting into or out of the policyholder's insured auto while being used by the policyholder, a resident relative, or any other person with the policyholder's permission.

Under Part 2, insured persons include any other person who sustains bodily injury while in, on, getting into or out of a non-owned auto if the injury results from the policyholder's operation or occupancy.

Under Part 2, insured persons include any other person who sustains bodily injury while in, on, getting into or out of a non-owned auto if the injury results from the operation on the policyholder's behalf by the policyholder's private chauffeur or domestic servant.

Under Part 2, insured persons include any other person who sustains bodily injury while in, on, getting into or out of a non-owned private passenger auto or trailer if the injury results from the operation or occupancy by a resident relative.
Under Part 2, the use of non-owned autos must be with the owner's permission.
Under Part 2, insured autos include any auto described on the Policy Declarations, including the four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto the policyholder replaces it with.

Under Part 2, insured autos include an additional four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto the policyholder becomes the owner of during the policy period, if Allstate insures all other private passenger autos or utility autos the policyholder owns.
Under Part 2, to cover an additional auto during the policy period, the policyholder must tell Allstate within 60 days of acquiring the auto and must pay any additional premium.

Under Part 2, insured autos include a substitute four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto, not owned by the policyholder or a resident, temporarily used while the policyholder's insured auto is being serviced or repaired, or if the policyholder's insured auto is stolen or destroyed.

Under Part 2, insured autos include a non-owned auto having not less than four wheels used with the owner's permission, provided this auto is not available or furnished for the regular use of an insured person.

Under Part 2, insured autos include a trailer while attached to an insured auto, designed for use with a private passenger auto or utility auto, not used for business purposes with other than a private passenger auto or utility auto.
Under Part 2, 'Allstate', 'We', 'Us', or 'Our' means the Allstate Insurance Company or Allstate Indemnity Company as shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 2, 'Auto' means a land motor vehicle designed for use on public roads.
Under Part 2, 'Resident' means a person who physically resides in the policyholder's household with the intention of continuing residence there.
Under Part 2, the policyholder's unmarried dependent children while temporarily away from home will be considered residents if they intend to resume residing in the policyholder's household.
Under Part 2, 'Utility Auto' means an auto of the pickup body, sedan delivery or panel truck type with a rated load capacity of not more than 2,000 pounds.
Under Part 2, 'You' or 'Your' means the policyholder named on the Policy Declarations and that policyholder's resident spouse.

Under Part 2 (Exclusion 1), coverage does not apply to the policyholder or a resident relative while in, on, getting into or out of an auto the policyholder or a resident relative own but do not insure for this coverage.

Under Part 2 (Exclusion 2a), coverage does not apply to the policyholder or a resident relative while in, on, getting into or out of, or struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle operated on rails or crawler-treads.

Under Part 2 (Exclusion 2b), coverage does not apply to the policyholder or a resident relative while in, on, getting into or out of, or struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle or other equipment designed for use off public roads, while not on public roads.
Under Part 2 (Exclusion 3a), coverage does not apply to any person while in, on, getting into or out of an owned auto while available for hire to the public, except for shared-expense car pools.
Under Part 2 (Exclusion 3b), coverage does not apply to any person while in, on, getting into or out of an auto or trailer while used as a residence or premises.
Under Part 2 (Exclusion 4a), coverage does not apply to any person other than the policyholder or a resident relative while using a non-owned auto which is available for hire by the public.

Under Part 2 (Exclusion 4b), coverage does not apply to any person other than the policyholder or a resident relative while using a non-owned auto in auto business operations such as repairing, servicing, testing, washing, parking, storing or selling of autos.
Under Part 2, coverage is provided for the policyholder, the policyholder's private chauffeur or domestic servant while using a private passenger auto or trailer in any other business or occupation.
Under Part 2 (Exclusion 5), coverage does not apply to any person resulting from any act of war, insurrection, rebellion, or revolution.
Under Part 2, the limit shown on the Policy Declarations is the maximum Allstate will pay for all expenses incurred by or for each insured person as the result of any one auto accident.
Under Part 2, the medical payments limit applies to each insured auto as stated on the Policy Declarations.

Under Part 2, the insuring of more than one person or auto under this policy will not increase Allstate's limit beyond the amount shown for any one auto, even though a separate premium is charged for each auto.
Under Part 2, the limit will not be increased if the policyholder has other auto insurance policies that apply.

Under Part 2, if an insured person dies as the result of a covered auto accident, Allstate will pay the least of the following as a funeral service expenses benefit: $2,000; or the Coverage CC limit of liability stated on the Policy Declarations; or the remaining portion of the Coverage CC limit of liability not expended for other covered medical expenses.
Under Part 2, the funeral service expenses benefit does not increase, and will not be paid in addition to, the limits of liability stated on the Policy Declarations for Coverage CC.
Under Part 2, the funeral service expenses benefit is payable to the deceased insured person's spouse if a resident of the same household at the time of the accident.
Under Part 2, if the deceased is a minor, the funeral service expenses benefit is payable to either parent if that parent is a resident of the same household at the time of the accident.
Under Part 2, in all other cases, the funeral service expenses benefit is payable to the deceased insured person's estate.
Under Part 2, there will be no duplication of payments made under the bodily injury liability and automobile medical payments coverages of this policy.
Under Part 2, all payments made to or on behalf of any person under Coverage CC will be considered as advance payments to that person.
Under Part 2, the damages payable under the bodily injury liability coverage of this policy will be reduced by the amount of Coverage CC advance payments.
Under Part 2, if the insured person incurs medical expenses which are unreasonable or unnecessary, Allstate may refuse to pay for those expenses and contest them.
Under Part 2, unreasonable medical expenses are fees for medical services which are substantially higher than the usual and customary charges for those services.

Under Part 2, unnecessary medical expenses are fees for medical services which are not usually and customarily performed for treatment of the injury, including fees for an excessive number, amount, or duration of medical services.

Under Part 2, if the insured person is sued by a medical services provider because Allstate refuses to pay contested medical expenses, Allstate will pay all defense costs and any resulting judgment against the insured person.
Under Part 2, Allstate will choose the counsel when defending against contested medical expense claims.
Under Part 2, the insured person must cooperate with Allstate in the defense of any claim or lawsuit related to contested medical expenses.
Under Part 2, if Allstate asks the insured person to attend hearings or trials related to contested medical expenses, Allstate will pay up to $50 per day for loss of wages or salary.
Under Part 2, Allstate will also pay other reasonable expenses incurred at Allstate's request related to contested medical expense defense.
Under Part 2, when Coverage CC applies to a substitute auto or non-owned auto, Allstate will pay only after all other collectible auto medical insurance has been exhausted.
Under Part 2, when Coverage CC applies to a replacement auto or additional auto, this policy will not apply if the policyholder has other collectible auto medical insurance.
Under Part 2, when Allstate asks, an insured person must cooperate with Allstate in the investigation, settlement and defense of any claim or lawsuit.
Under Part 2, Allstate cannot be obligated if an insured person voluntarily takes any action or makes any payments other than for covered expenses for first aid to others.
Under Part 2, no one may sue Allstate under this coverage unless there is full compliance with all the policy terms.
Under Part 2, as soon as possible, any person making claim must give Allstate written proof of claim, including all details Allstate may need to determine the amounts payable.
Under Part 2, Allstate may require any person making claim to submit to questioning under oath and sign the transcript.
Under Part 2, the injured person may be required to take medical examinations by physicians Allstate chooses, as often as Allstate reasonably requires.
Under Part 2, Allstate must be given authorization to obtain medical reports and other records pertinent to the claim.

Under Part 3 (Coverage CM - Automobile Death Indemnity Insurance), Allstate will pay the benefit shown on the Policy Declarations if an insured person dies as a direct result of bodily injury, sickness or disease caused by an auto accident.
Under Part 3, the injury must be sustained while the insured person is in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by an auto, trailer or semi-trailer.
Under Part 3, benefits will be paid only if death occurs within 90 days of the auto accident.

Under Part 3, benefits will be paid only if death occurs within 1 year of the auto accident and the bodily injury has continuously prevented the insured person from performing all duties pertaining to that person's occupation.
Under Part 3, insured persons are the person or persons shown as insured on the Policy Declarations under Coverage CM.
Under Part 3, 'Allstate', 'We', or 'Us' means the Allstate Insurance Company or Allstate Indemnity Company as shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 3, 'Auto' means a land motor vehicle designed for use on public roads.

Under Part 3 (Exclusion 1a), coverage does not apply to death sustained in the course of an occupation by any person while operating, loading, unloading, assisting on, or performing any other duties related to the use of a commercial auto, or an auto hired or rented to others for a charge.
Under Part 3 (Exclusion 1b), coverage does not apply to death sustained in the course of an occupation by any person while repairing or servicing autos, including any related duties.
Under Part 3 (Exclusion 2), coverage does not apply to death due to suicide committed while sane or insane.
Under Part 3 (Exclusion 3), coverage does not apply to death due to any act of war, insurrection, rebellion, or revolution.
Under Part 3 (Exclusion 4a), coverage does not apply to death sustained while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle operated on rails or crawler-treads.

Under Part 3 (Exclusion 4b), coverage does not apply to death sustained while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle or other equipment designed for use off public roads, while not on public roads.
Under Part 3 (Exclusion 4c), coverage does not apply to death sustained while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle when used as a residence or premises.
Under Part 3, the death benefit is payable to the deceased insured person's spouse, who must be a resident of the same household as the insured person at the time of the accident.
Under Part 3, if the deceased is a minor, the benefit is payable to either parent who must be a resident of the same household as the minor at the time of the accident.
Under Part 3, in all other cases, the benefit is payable to the deceased insured person's estate.
Under Part 3, Allstate has the right and must be given the opportunity to make an autopsy where it is not prohibited by law.
Under Part 3, the beneficiary's consent is not required for cancellation, assignment, change of beneficiary, or any other change under this coverage.
Under Part 3, as soon as possible, Allstate must be given written proof of claim, including all details Allstate may need to determine if benefits are payable.
Under Part 3, Allstate must be given authorization to obtain medical reports and copies of records pertinent to the claim.

Under Part 4 (Coverage CW - Automobile Disability Income Protection), Allstate will pay the weekly benefit shown on the Policy Declarations if an insured person sustains continuous total disability as a direct result of bodily injury, sickness or disease caused by an auto accident.
Under Part 4, the injury must be sustained while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by an auto, trailer or semi-trailer.
Under Part 4, benefits will be paid only while the insured person is alive and only if the disability commences within 20 days of the date of the accident.

Under Part 4, benefits will be paid only while the insured person is alive and only if during the first year after commencement, the disability continuously prevents the insured person from performing all duties pertaining to that person's occupation.

Under Part 4, benefits will be paid only while the insured person is alive and only if during the second and subsequent years after commencement, the disability continuously prevents the insured person from engaging in any occupation or employment for wage or profit.
Under Part 4, insured persons are the person or persons shown as insured on the Policy Declarations under Coverage CW.
Under Part 4, 'Allstate', 'We', or 'Us' means the Allstate Insurance Company or Allstate Indemnity Company as shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 4, 'Auto' means a land motor vehicle designed for use on public roads.

Under Part 4 (Exclusion 1a), coverage does not apply to disability sustained in the course of an occupation by any person while operating, loading, unloading, assisting on, or performing any other duties related to the use of a commercial auto, or an auto hired or rented to others for a charge.
Under Part 4 (Exclusion 1b), coverage does not apply to disability sustained in the course of an occupation by any person while repairing or servicing autos, including any related duties.
Under Part 4 (Exclusion 2), coverage does not apply to disability due to any attempt at suicide while sane or insane.
Under Part 4 (Exclusion 3), coverage does not apply to disability due to any act of war, insurrection, rebellion or revolution.
Under Part 4 (Exclusion 4a), coverage does not apply to disability sustained while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle operated on rails or crawler-treads.

Under Part 4 (Exclusion 4b), coverage does not apply to disability sustained while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle or other equipment designed for use off public roads, while not on public roads.
Under Part 4 (Exclusion 4c), coverage does not apply to disability sustained while in, on, getting into or out of, or when struck as a pedestrian by a vehicle when used as a residence or premises.
Under Part 4, weekly benefits are payable to the disabled insured person.
Under Part 4, accrued weekly benefits are payable every four weeks.
Under Part 4, any remaining balance is payable at termination of the disability period.
Under Part 4, benefits end upon the death of the insured person.
Under Part 4, as soon as possible, any person making claim must give Allstate written proof of claim.
Under Part 4, the injured person may be required to take medical examinations by physicians Allstate chooses, as often as Allstate reasonably requires.
Under Part 4, Allstate must be given authorization to obtain medical reports and copies of records pertinent to the claim.

Under Part 5 (Coverage SS - Uninsured Motorists Insurance), Allstate will pay damages because of bodily injury which an insured person is legally entitled to recover from the owner or operator of an uninsured auto.
Under Part 5, bodily injury must be caused by accident and arise out of the ownership, maintenance or use of an uninsured auto.
Under Part 5, Allstate will not pay any punitive or exemplary damages.
Under Part 5, the right to benefits and the amount payable will be decided by agreement between the insured person and Allstate.
Under Part 5, if an agreement cannot be reached, the decision may be made by arbitration.

Under Part 5, if an insured person sues a person believed responsible for the accident without giving Allstate notice so as to give Allstate adequate time to intervene in a lawsuit, Allstate is not bound by any resulting judgment.
Under Part 5, insured persons include the policyholder and any relative who resides in the policyholder's household.
Under Part 5, insured persons include any other person while in, on, getting into or out of the policyholder's insured auto with the policyholder's permission.

Under Part 5, insured persons include any other person who is legally entitled to recover because of bodily injury to the policyholder, a relative who resides in the policyholder's household, or an occupant of the policyholder's insured auto with the policyholder's permission.
Under Part 5, an insured auto is a motor vehicle described on the Policy Declarations and the motor vehicle the policyholder replaces it with.

Under Part 5, an insured auto includes a motor vehicle the policyholder acquires ownership of during the policy period, if Allstate insures all other private passenger motor vehicles the policyholder owns, and the policyholder notifies Allstate within 60 days after acquisition and pays any additional premium.

Under Part 5, an insured auto includes a motor vehicle not owned by the policyholder or a relative who resides in the policyholder's household, being temporarily used while the policyholder's insured auto is being serviced or repaired, or if the policyholder's insured auto is stolen or destroyed, used with the owner's permission and not furnished for regular use.

Under Part 5, an insured auto includes a motor vehicle not owned by the policyholder or a relative who resides in the policyholder's household, being operated by the policyholder with the owner's permission, not furnished for the regular use of the policyholder or any relative who resides in the policyholder's household.
Under Part 5, an insured auto is not made available for public hire by an insured person.
Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is a motor vehicle which has no bodily injury liability bond or insurance policy in effect at the time of the accident.
Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is a motor vehicle for which the insurer denies coverage, or the insurer becomes insolvent.

Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is a hit-and-run motor vehicle which causes bodily injury to an insured person by physical contact with the insured person or with a vehicle occupied by that person, where the identity of the operator and the owner of the vehicle must be unknown or cannot be found.
Under Part 5, for a hit-and-run vehicle to qualify, the accident must be reported as soon as possible to the proper authorities.
Under Part 5, for a hit-and-run vehicle to qualify, Allstate must be notified within 30 days.
Under Part 5, if the insured person was occupying a vehicle at the time of a hit-and-run accident, Allstate has the right to inspect it.

Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is an underinsured motor vehicle which has liability protection in effect and applicable at the time of the accident but less than the applicable damages the insured person is legally entitled to recover.
Under Part 5, an underinsured motor vehicle is not an insured auto as defined under this coverage.
Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is a motor vehicle for which no evidence of financial responsibility is supplied to the Department of Motor Vehicles within 60 days after the accident occurs.
Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is not a motor vehicle that is lawfully self-insured.
Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is not a motor vehicle owned by any federal, state, or local government or agency.
Under Part 5, an uninsured auto is not a motor vehicle insured for bodily injury under Part 1 of this policy.
Under Part 5, 'Allstate', 'We', 'Us', or 'Our' means the Allstate Insurance Company or Allstate Indemnity Company as shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 5, 'Bodily Injury' means bodily injury, sickness, disease, or death.

Under Part 5, 'Motor Vehicle' means a land motor vehicle or trailer other than: a vehicle or other equipment designed for use off public roads while not on public roads; a vehicle operated on rails or crawler-treads; or a vehicle when used as a residence or premises.
Under Part 5, 'Resident' or 'Reside' means a person who physically resides in the policyholder's household with the intention of continuing residence there.
Under Part 5, the policyholder's unmarried dependent children while temporarily away from home will be considered residents if they intend to resume residing in the policyholder's household.
Under Part 5, 'You' or 'Your' means the insured person named on the Policy Declarations and that insured person's resident spouse.
Under Part 5 (Exclusion 1), Allstate will not pay any damages to any person who makes a settlement without Allstate's written consent.

Under Part 5 (Exclusion 2), Allstate will not pay any damages for bodily injury to anyone while in, on, getting into or out of a motor vehicle the policyholder owns which is insured for this coverage under another policy.

Under Part 5 (Exclusion 3), Allstate will not pay any damages for bodily injury if the payment would directly or indirectly benefit any workers compensation or disability benefits insurer including a self insurer.

Under Part 5, the 'each person' coverage limit is the maximum that Allstate will pay for damages arising out of bodily injury to one person in any one motor vehicle accident, including damages sustained by anyone else as a result of that bodily injury.

Under Part 5, the 'each accident' coverage limit is the maximum that Allstate will pay for damages arising out of bodily injury to two or more persons in any one motor vehicle accident, subject to the limit for each person.

Under Part 5, the limits are the maximum Allstate will pay for any one motor vehicle accident regardless of the number of claims made, vehicles or persons shown on the Policy Declarations, or vehicles involved in the accident.

Under Part 5, damages payable will be reduced by all amounts paid by the owner or operator of the uninsured auto or anyone else responsible, including all sums paid under the bodily injury liability coverage of this or any other auto policy.

Under Part 5, damages payable will be reduced by all amounts payable under any workers compensation law, disability benefits law, or similar law, automobile medical payments, or any similar automobile medical payments coverage.

Under Part 5, Allstate is not obligated to make any payment for bodily injury which arises out of the use of an underinsured motor vehicle until after the limits of liability for all liability protection in effect and applicable at the time of the accident have been exhausted by payment of judgments or settlements.
Under Part 5, if the insured person was in, on, getting into or out of a vehicle the policyholder does not own which is insured for this coverage under another policy, this coverage will be excess.

Under Part 5, when coverage is excess and the insured person is legally entitled to recover damages in excess of the other policy limit, Allstate will pay up to the policyholder's policy limit, but only after the other insurance has been exhausted.

Under Part 5, if more than one policy applies to the accident on a primary basis, the total benefits payable to any one person will not exceed the maximum benefits payable by the policy with the highest limit for uninsured motorists coverage.

Under Part 5, Allstate will bear its proportionate share with other uninsured motorists benefits, regardless of how many autos or auto policies may be involved, whether written by Allstate or another company.

Under Part 5, as soon as possible, the policyholder or any other person making claim must give Allstate written proof of claim, including all details reasonably required by Allstate to determine the amounts payable.
Under Part 5, Allstate may require any person making claim to submit to questioning under oath and sign the transcript.
Under Part 5, the insured person may be required to take medical examinations by physicians selected by Allstate, as often as Allstate reasonably requires.
Under Part 5, the insured person or that person's representative must authorize Allstate to obtain medical reports and copies of records.
Under Part 5, Allstate may require the insured person to take proper action to preserve all rights to recover damages from anyone responsible for the bodily injury.

Under Part 5 (Trust Agreement), when Allstate pays any person under this coverage, Allstate is entitled to repayment of amounts paid by Allstate and related collection expenses out of the proceeds of any settlement or judgment that person recovers from any responsible party or insurer.
Under Part 5 (Trust Agreement), all rights of recovery against any responsible party or insurer must be maintained and preserved for Allstate's benefit.
Under Part 5 (Trust Agreement), if Allstate asks, insured persons must take proper action in their name to recover damages from any responsible party or insurer.
Under Part 5 (Trust Agreement), Allstate will select the attorney for such recovery actions.
Under Part 5 (Trust Agreement), Allstate will pay all related costs and fees for such recovery actions.
Under Part 5, Allstate will not ask the insured person to sue the insured of an insolvent insurer.
Under Part 5, any amount due is payable to the insured person, to the parent or guardian of an injured minor, or to the spouse of any insured person who dies.
Under Part 5, Allstate may pay any person or estate lawfully entitled to recover the damages.
Under Part 5, no one may sue Allstate under this coverage unless there is full compliance with all the policy terms.

Under Part 5, if the insured person and Allstate do not agree on the insured person's right to receive damages on any claim involving an amount of $3,000 or less, the dispute will be settled by arbitration.

Under Part 5, if the insured person and Allstate do not agree on the insured person's right to receive damages on any claim involving an amount over $3,000, the disagreement may be settled by arbitration upon the mutual consent of the insured person and Allstate.
Under Part 5, if the insured person and Allstate do not mutually agree to arbitrate a claim over $3,000, the disagreement will be resolved in a court of competent jurisdiction.
Under Part 5, unless the insured person or Allstate objects, arbitration will take place under the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

Under Part 5, if either party objects to the rules of the American Arbitration Association, the insured person will select one arbitrator and Allstate will select another, and the two arbitrators will select a third.

Under Part 5, if the two arbitrators cannot agree on a third arbitrator within 30 days, the judge of the court of record in the county of jurisdiction where arbitration is pending will appoint the third arbitrator.
Under Part 5, the written decision of any two arbitrators will determine the issues.
Under Part 5, local rules governing evidence and procedure will apply in arbitration.
Under Part 5, the insured person will pay the arbitrator that person selects, and Allstate will pay the one Allstate selects.
Under Part 5, the expense of the third arbitrator and all other expenses of arbitration will be shared equally.
Under Part 5, attorney fees and fees paid to medical or other expert witnesses are not considered arbitration expenses and are to be paid by the party incurring them.
Under Part 5, regardless of the method of arbitration, either party has a right to a trial on all issues in a court of competent jurisdiction.
Under Part 5, costs, including attorney fees for trial, are to be paid by the party incurring them.

Under Part 6 (Coverage DD - Auto Collision Insurance), Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss to the policyholder's insured auto or a non-owned auto (including insured loss to an attached trailer) from a collision with another object or by upset of that auto or trailer.
Under Part 6 (Coverage DD), the deductible amount will not be subtracted from the loss payment in collisions involving the policyholder's insured auto and another auto insured by Allstate.

Under Part 6 (Coverage DE - Diminishing Deductible Auto Collision Insurance), if the loss is $100 or more, Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss to the policyholder's insured auto or a non-owned auto (including insured loss to an attached trailer) from a collision with another object or by upset of that auto or trailer, with no deductible applying to losses in excess of $100.

Under Part 6 (Coverage DE), if the loss is between $50 and $100, the policyholder pays the difference between the amount of loss and $100, and Allstate will pay the rest of the loss up to Allstate's limits of liability.
Under Part 6 (Coverage DE), if the loss is $50 or less, Allstate will not make any payment.

Under Part 6 (Coverage DE), the deductible amount will not be subtracted from the loss payment in a collision involving the policyholder's insured auto and another auto insured by Allstate, even if the loss is $50 or less.
Under Part 6 (Coverage HH - Auto Comprehensive Insurance), Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss to the policyholder's insured auto or a non-owned auto not caused by collision.

Under Part 6 (Coverage HH), losses covered include those caused by: missiles, falling objects, fire, theft or larceny, explosion, earthquake, windstorm, hail, water, flood, malicious mischief or vandalism, and riot or civil commotion.
Under Part 6 (Coverage HH), glass breakage, whether or not caused by collision, and collision with a bird or animal is covered.
Under Part 6 (Coverage HH), the deductible amount will not be subtracted from the loss payment when the loss is caused by a peril listed under Coverage HE.
Under Part 6 (Coverage HH), by agreement between the policyholder and Allstate, the deductible amount will not be subtracted from a glass breakage loss if the glass is repaired rather than replaced.

Under Part 6 (Coverage HE - Auto Fire, Lightning and Transportation Insurance), Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss to the policyholder's insured auto or a non-owned auto due to fire or lightning.

Under Part 6 (Coverage HE), Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss due to smoke or smudge from a sudden, unusual and faulty operation of any fixed heating equipment serving the premises in which the auto is located.

Under Part 6 (Coverage HE), Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss due to stranding, sinking, burning, collision or derailment of any conveyance in or upon which the auto is being transported on land or on water.
Under Part 6 (Coverage HF - Auto Theft Insurance), Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss to the policyholder's insured auto or a non-owned auto caused by theft or larceny.

Under Part 6 (Coverage HG - Auto Fire, Lightning, Transportation and Theft Insurance), Allstate will pay for direct and accidental loss to the policyholder's insured auto or a non-owned auto caused by any peril listed under Coverages HE or HF.
Under Part 6 (Coverage JJ - Towing And Labor Costs), Allstate will pay costs for labor done at the initial place of disablement of the policyholder's insured auto or a non-owned auto.
Under Part 6 (Coverage JJ), Allstate will also pay for towing made necessary by the disablement.
Under Part 6 (Coverage JJ), the total limit of Allstate's liability for each loss is shown on the Policy Declarations.

Under Part 6 (Coverage UU - Rental Reimbursement Coverage), if the policyholder has collision or comprehensive coverage under this policy and the loss involves either coverage, Allstate will repay the policyholder for the cost of renting an auto from a rental agency or garage.
Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), Allstate will not pay more than the dollar amount per day shown on the Policy Declarations for rental reimbursement.
Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), Allstate will not pay mileage charges for rental reimbursement.

Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), if the policyholder's insured auto is stolen, payment for transportation expenses will be made under the terms of paragraph 3 of 'Additional Payments Allstate Will Make', but the Coverage UU limits will apply if they exceed the limits stated under that paragraph.
Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), if the policyholder's insured auto is disabled by a collision or comprehensive loss, rental coverage starts the day after the loss.
Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), if the auto is driveable, rental coverage starts the day after the auto is taken to the garage for repairs.
Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), coverage ends when the first of the following occurs: if the auto is disabled by a collision or comprehensive loss, completion of repairs or replacement of the auto.
Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), coverage ends when the first of the following occurs: if the auto is stolen, when Allstate offers settlement or the auto is returned to use.
Under Part 6 (Coverage UU), coverage ends when the first of the following occurs: thirty full days of coverage.

Under Part 6 (Coverage ZA - Sound System Coverage), Allstate will pay for loss to a sound system permanently installed in the policyholder's auto by bolts, brackets or other means, its antennas or other apparatus in or on the policyholder's auto used specifically with that system.
Under Part 6 (Coverage ZA), Coverage ZA applies only if comprehensive insurance is in effect under this policy.

Under Part 6 (Coverage ZA), this coverage makes sound systems, and antennas or other apparatus used specifically with them, insured property under the terms of both collision and comprehensive insurance.
Under Part 6 (Coverage ZA), the limit of Allstate's liability is shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 6 (Coverage ZZ - Tape Coverage), Allstate will pay for loss to any tapes or similar items used with any auto sound systems.
Under Part 6 (Coverage ZZ), coverage applies to property the policyholder or a resident relative owns that is in or on the policyholder's insured auto at the time of loss.
Under Part 6 (Coverage ZZ), the total limit of Allstate's liability for each loss is shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 6 (Coverage ZZ), this coverage applies only if the policyholder has comprehensive insurance under this policy.
Under Part 6 (Coverage ZZ), Coverage ZZ makes tapes or similar items insured property under the policyholder's comprehensive insurance.

Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), Allstate will pay up to $200 for loss of clothing and personal luggage, including its contents, belonging to the policyholder or a resident relative while it is in or upon the policyholder's insured auto.
Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the $200 clothing/luggage provision does not apply if the insured auto is a travel-trailer.
Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the clothing/luggage coverage applies only when: the loss is caused by collision and the policyholder has purchased collision insurance.
Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the clothing/luggage coverage applies only when: the entire auto is stolen, and the policyholder has purchased comprehensive insurance.

Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the clothing/luggage coverage applies only when: physical damage is done to the auto and to the clothing and luggage caused by earthquake, explosion, falling objects, fire, lightning, or flood and the policyholder has purchased comprehensive insurance.

Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), Allstate will repay the policyholder up to $10 for the cost of transportation from the place of theft of the policyholder's insured auto or disablement of the auto to the policyholder's destination.
Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the $10 transportation reimbursement applies if the entire auto is stolen and the policyholder has comprehensive coverage under this policy.

Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the $10 transportation reimbursement applies if the auto is disabled by a collision or comprehensive loss, and the policyholder has the applicable coverage under this policy.
Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the $10 transportation reimbursement provision does not apply if the insured auto is a travel-trailer.

Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), if the policyholder has comprehensive insurance under this policy, Allstate will repay up to $10 a day but not more than $300 for each loss for the cost of transportation when the entire auto is stolen.

Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), the transportation reimbursement when auto is stolen begins 48 hours after the policyholder reports the theft to Allstate, but ends when Allstate offers settlement or the auto is returned to use.

Under Part 6 (Additional Payments), if the policyholder has purchased collision or comprehensive insurance under this policy, Allstate will pay general average and salvage charges imposed when the policyholder's insured auto is being transported.

Under Part 6 (Insured Autos), any auto described on the Policy Declarations is an insured auto, including the four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto the policyholder replaces it with if the policyholder notifies Allstate within 60 days of the replacement and pays the additional premium.

Under Part 6 (Insured Autos), an additional four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto the policyholder becomes the owner of during the policy period is an insured auto if Allstate insures all other private passenger autos or utility autos the policyholder owns, and the policyholder tells Allstate within 60 days of acquiring the auto and pays any additional premium.

Under Part 6 (Insured Autos), a substitute four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto not owned by the policyholder or a resident, temporarily used with the permission of the owner while the policyholder's insured auto is being serviced or repaired, or if the policyholder's insured auto is stolen or destroyed, is an insured auto.

Under Part 6 (Insured Autos), a non-owned four wheel private passenger auto or utility auto used by the policyholder or a resident relative with the owner's permission, not available or furnished for the regular use of the policyholder or any resident, is an insured auto.

Under Part 6 (Insured Autos), a trailer while attached to an insured auto, designed for use with a private passenger auto, not used for business purposes with other than a private passenger auto or utility auto, is an insured auto.
Under Part 6 (Insured Autos), home, office, store, display, or passenger trailers, travel-trailers or camper units are not covered unless described on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 6, 'Allstate', 'We', 'Us', or 'Our' means the Allstate Insurance Company or Allstate Indemnity Company as shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 6, 'Auto' means a land motor vehicle designed for use on public roads.
Under Part 6, 'Camper Unit' means a demountable unit designed to be used as temporary living quarters, including all equipment and accessories built into and forming a permanent part of the unit.
Under Part 6, a camper unit does not include caps, tops or canopies designed for use as protection of the cargo area of a utility auto.
Under Part 6, a camper unit does not include radio or television antennas, awnings, cabanas, or equipment designed to create additional off highway living facilities.
Under Part 6, 'Motor Home' means a self-propelled vehicle equipped, designed or used as a living quarters.
Under Part 6, 'Resident' means a person who physically resides in the policyholder's household with the intention of continuing residence there.
Under Part 6, the policyholder's unmarried dependent children while temporarily away from home will be considered residents if they intend to resume residing in the policyholder's household.

Under Part 6, 'Sound System' means any device within the insured auto designed for voice or video transmission, or for voice, video or radar signal reception; or recording or playing back recorded material; or supplying power to cellular or similar telephone equipment, and which is installed in a location other than the one designed by the auto's manufacturer for that device.
Under Part 6, 'Travel-trailer' means a trailer of the house, cabin or camping type equipped or used as a living quarters.
Under Part 6, 'Utility Auto' means an auto of the pick-up body, sedan delivery or panel truck type with a rated load capacity of not more than 2,000 pounds.
Under Part 6, 'You' or 'Your' means the policyholder named on the Policy Declarations and that policyholder's resident spouse.
Under Part 6 (Exclusion 1), these coverages do not apply to loss caused intentionally by or at the direction of an insured person.
Under Part 6 (Exclusion 2), these coverages do not apply to any auto used for the transportation of people or property for a fee, except for shared-expense car pools.
Under Part 6 (Exclusion 3), these coverages do not apply to any damage or loss resulting from any act of war, insurrection, rebellion or revolution.

Under Part 6 (Exclusion 4), these coverages do not apply to loss to any non-owned auto used in auto business operations such as repairing, servicing, testing, washing, parking, storing or selling of autos.
Under Part 6 (Exclusion 5), these coverages do not apply to loss due to radioactive contamination.

Under Part 6 (Exclusion 6), these coverages do not apply to damage resulting from wear and tear, freezing, mechanical or electrical breakdown unless the damage is the burning of wiring used to connect electrical components, or the result of other loss covered by this policy.

Under Part 6 (Exclusion 7), these coverages do not apply to tires unless stolen or damaged by fire, malicious mischief or vandalism, or if the damage to tires occurs at the same time and from the same cause as other loss covered by this policy.

Under Part 6 (Exclusion 8), these coverages do not apply to loss to any sound system within the policyholder's auto, or any apparatus in or on the auto designed for use with that system, unless the policyholder has purchased Coverage ZA.
Under Part 6 (Exclusion 9), these coverages do not apply to loss to any tapes or similar items, unless the policyholder has Coverage ZZ under this policy.
Under Part 6 (Exclusion 10), these coverages do not apply to loss to a camper unit whether or not mounted, unless the camper unit is described on the Policy Declarations.

Under Part 6 (Exclusion 11), these coverages do not apply to loss to appliances, furniture, equipment and accessories that are not built into or forming a permanent part of a motor home or travel-trailer.

Under Part 6 (Exclusion 12), these coverages do not apply to loss to the policyholder's motor home or the policyholder's travel-trailer while rented to anyone else unless a specific premium is shown on the Policy Declarations for the rented vehicle.
Under Part 6 (Right To Appraisal), both the policyholder and Allstate have a right to demand an appraisal of the loss.
Under Part 6 (Right To Appraisal), each party (policyholder and Allstate) will appoint and pay a qualified appraiser.
Under Part 6 (Right To Appraisal), other appraisal expenses will be shared equally.
Under Part 6 (Right To Appraisal), the two appraisers, or a judge of a court of record, will choose an umpire.
Under Part 6 (Right To Appraisal), each appraiser will state the actual cash value and the amount of loss, and if they disagree, they will submit their differences to the umpire.
Under Part 6 (Right To Appraisal), a written decision by any two of the three persons (two appraisers and umpire) will determine the amount of the loss.
Under Part 6 (Payment Of Loss), Allstate may pay for the loss in money, or may repair or replace the damaged or stolen property.

Under Part 6 (Payment Of Loss), Allstate may, at any time before the loss is paid or the property is replaced, return at Allstate's own expense any stolen property, either to the policyholder or at Allstate's option to the address shown on the Policy Declarations, with payment for any resulting damage.
Under Part 6 (Payment Of Loss), Allstate may take all or part of the property at the agreed or appraised value.
Under Part 6 (Payment Of Loss), Allstate may settle any claim or loss either with the policyholder or the owner of the property.
Under Part 6 (Limits Of Liability), Allstate's limit of liability is the actual cash value of the property or damaged part of the property at the time of loss.
Under Part 6 (Limits Of Liability), the actual cash value will be reduced by the deductible for each coverage as shown on the Policy Declarations.
Under Part 6 (Limits Of Liability), Allstate's liability will not exceed what it would cost to repair or replace the property or part with other of like kind and quality.
Under Part 6 (Limits Of Liability), the limit for loss to any covered trailer not described on the Policy Declarations is $500.
Under Part 6 (Limits Of Liability), an auto and attached trailer are considered separate autos, and the policyholder must pay the deductible, if any, on each.
Under Part 6 (Limits Of Liability), only one deductible will apply to an auto with a mounted camper unit.
Under Part 6 (Limits Of Liability), if the camper unit is unmounted, a separate deductible will apply to the auto and camper unit.

Under Part 6 (If There Is Other Insurance), if there is other insurance covering the loss at the time of the accident, Allstate will pay only its share of any damages, determined by adding the limits of this insurance to the limits of all other insurance that applies on the same basis and finding the percentage of the total that Allstate's limits represent.
Under Part 6 (If There Is Other Insurance), when this insurance covers a substitute auto or non-owned auto, Allstate will pay only after all other collectible insurance has been exhausted.
Under Part 6 (If There Is Other Insurance), when this insurance covers a replacement auto or additional auto, this policy will not apply if the policyholder has other collectible insurance.

Under Part 6 (If There Is Other Insurance), when more than one coverage is applicable to the loss, the policyholder may recover under the broadest coverage but not both; however, any Coverage ZA deductible will always apply.
Under Part 6 (Action Against Allstate), no one may sue Allstate under this coverage unless there is full compliance with all the policy terms.
Under Part 6 (Subrogation Rights), when Allstate pays, the policyholder's rights of recovery from anyone else become Allstate's up to the amount Allstate has paid.
Under Part 6 (Subrogation Rights), the policyholder must protect these rights and help Allstate enforce them.
Under Part 6 (What You Must Do If There Is A Loss), as soon as possible, any person making claim must give Allstate written proof of loss, including all details reasonably required by Allstate.
Under Part 6 (What You Must Do If There Is A Loss), Allstate has the right to inspect the damaged property.
Under Part 6 (What You Must Do If There Is A Loss), Allstate may require any person making claim to file with Allstate a sworn proof of loss.
Under Part 6 (What You Must Do If There Is A Loss), Allstate may also require that person to submit to examinations under oath.
Under Part 6 (What You Must Do If There Is A Loss), the policyholder must protect the auto from further loss, and Allstate will pay reasonable expenses to guard against further loss.
Under Part 6 (What You Must Do If There Is A Loss), if the policyholder does not protect the auto, further loss is not covered.
Under Part 6 (What You Must Do If There Is A Loss), all theft losses must be reported promptly to the police.
Allstate has caused this policy to be signed by its Secretary and its President at Northbrook, Illinois.
If required by state law, this policy shall not be binding unless countersigned on the Policy Declarations by an authorized agent of Allstate.
The policy is signed by the Secretary of Allstate.
The policy is signed by the President, Personal Lines of Allstate.
